body:
## Updated scope (2026-04-14) — two concerns, one issue

This issue tracks **both** internal API consolidation **and** the JIE ↔ wfd-os integration boundary. Ritu's product-architecture review (2026-04-14) locked the JIE side as an API boundary — so this issue now covers both.

### Concern 1 — Internal wfd-os service consolidation (original scope)

Currently wfd-os has 10+ separate Python services (FastAPI + aiohttp) running on 10 different ports (8001-8011, 3978-3979, 7071). Each service opens its own DB connection (no pooling), has no auth middleware, no rate limiting, and hardcodes other services' ports.

62+ endpoints with no centralized control. This makes production deployment fragile and insecure.

Proposed: single FastAPI app with APIRouter per domain.

What changes:
- One app, one port (8000), one nginx rewrite
- Centralized auth middleware (check token before any handler)
- Centralized rate limiting (per-user, per-endpoint)
- SQLAlchemy connection pool (shared engine per tenant DB — see #13 for data isolation)
- LLM endpoints behind separate auth tier (staff-only; see #13)

Router migration:
- agents/portal/student_api.py (8001) → gateway/routers/student.py
- agents/portal/showcase_api.py (8002) → gateway/routers/showcase.py
- agents/portal/consulting_api.py (8003) → gateway/routers/consulting.py
- agents/portal/college_api.py (8004) → gateway/routers/college.py
- agents/portal/wji_api.py (8007) → gateway/routers/wji.py
- agents/marketing/api.py (8008) → gateway/routers/marketing.py
- agents/assistant/api.py (8009) → gateway/routers/assistant.py
- agents/apollo/api.py (8010) → gateway/routers/apollo.py
- agents/reporting/api.py (8000) → gateway/routers/reporting.py

next.config.mjs: 9 rewrites → 1 rewrite to localhost:8000

Why FastAPI over NestJS: agents are already Python (Gemini/Anthropic SDKs, psycopg2, Graph API). Rewriting in TypeScript adds translation overhead with no functional benefit.

**Caveat (from product-architecture review):** Each of these consolidated routers is potentially a sellable product on its own. Consolidating the transport layer (one FastAPI app, one port) is fine — but keep router modules modular enough that they can be extracted back into standalone services if a client wants to buy just "Talent Showcase" or "College Pipeline". See partner decisions on pip packaging (JIE repo) and product architecture doc.

### Concern 2 — JIE ↔ wfd-os integration is an API, not shared DB views (locked 2026-04-14)

Ritu locked the JIE integration pattern: wfd-os consumes JIE output via an **API boundary**, not shared DB views or event streams.

Her rationale: "Shared DB views couple the schema and that gets painful fast."

What this means:
- The current shared `skills` table in `talent_finder` DB is a short-term coupling to be replaced.
- JIE exposes a versioned HTTP API (e.g., `GET /v1/jobs`, `GET /v1/skills/{id}/demand`, `GET /v1/roles`).
- wfd-os backends call JIE over HTTP, cache responses as needed, and do NOT read JIE tables directly.
- JIE publishes its own pip-installable client (see companion issue in JIE repo — public API surface).

### Out of scope / tracked elsewhere

- JIE pip packaging → tracked in `job-intelligence-engine` repo
- JIE public API surface (endpoint list, schemas, auth model) → tracked in `job-intelligence-engine` repo
- Per-tenant DB isolation → #13
- White-label tenant resolution → #16
- Auth + magic links → #14
title: refactor: consolidate wfd-os microservices + lock JIE API boundary
